tasks.title: Add the build schemas foundation (BuildTaskStatus, common base, BuildTestVerdict)
tasks.summary: Create src/workflow/runners/build/schemas.ts with the purely-additive base types the rest of s3 builds on: the BuildTaskStatus string-literal union ('completed' | 'failed' | 'blocked' | 'not-reached'), the BuildTaskCommon base (taskId, title, dependsOn, note?), and BuildTestVerdict ({ command, passed, exitCode, summary }, all readonly). BuildTestVerdict is the daemon's own authoritative per-Task verdict produced by running the test command extracted verbatim from the PlanTask; it appears only on the reached arm added in t2. No existing symbol is touched (migration step 1).
acceptanceChecks: src/workflow/runners/build/schemas.ts exports BuildTaskStatus ('completed' | 'failed' | 'blocked' | 'not-reached'), a BuildTaskCommon base (taskId, title, dependsOn, optional note), and BuildTestVerdict ({ command, passed, exitCode, summary }) with every field readonly.
The module is purely additive — `npm run build` (tsc) passes and no existing exported symbol is modified.

tests.name: schemas: a constructed BuildTestVerdict carries { command, passed, exitCode, summary } and BuildTaskStatus admits exactly the four literals ('completed' | 'failed' | 'blocked' | 'not-reached'), all fields readonly

tasks.title: Add sc4 BuildTaskOutcome as the status-discriminated union (a2 reshape)
tasks.summary: Define BuildTaskOutcome = BuildTaskReached | BuildTaskUnreached per the winning a2 alternative and the accompanying hld.amendmentProposal (sharedContract.fieldRemove testVerdict, breaking:true). The reached arm (status 'completed' | 'failed') REQUIRES a daemon-produced testVerdict: BuildTestVerdict plus filesTouched and attempts; the unreached arm (status 'blocked' | 'not-reached') carries none of them because nothing ran. This encodes sc4's load-bearing invariant — testVerdict/filesTouched are daemon-produced, never self-reported — at the type level so a blocked/not-reached Task literally has no field in which a fabricated verdict could sit (migration step 2).
acceptanceChecks: BuildTaskOutcome is exported as `BuildTaskReached | BuildTaskUnreached`; the reached arm (status 'completed' | 'failed') requires testVerdict: BuildTestVerdict plus filesTouched and attempts, and the unreached arm (status 'blocked' | 'not-reached') declares none of those fields.
Constructing a 'blocked'/'not-reached' outcome carrying a testVerdict or filesTouched is a compile error, proving the daemon-produced invariant is enforced by the union rather than only documented.

tests.name: schemas: BuildTaskOutcome union invariant — a reached outcome ('completed'|'failed') requires testVerdict/filesTouched/attempts while a 'blocked'/'not-reached' outcome carrying testVerdict or filesTouched is a compile error

tasks.title: Add the sc5 seam types (TaskImplementerRequest / Report / Adapter)
tasks.summary: Add the three sc5 interfaces to schemas.ts: TaskImplementerRequest (task bound to the WORKFLOW PlanTask at src/workflow/artifacts/plan.ts — NOT the same-named analyze-types interface — plus storyDesignMarkdown, planMarkdown, completedDependencies: readonly BuildTaskOutcome[], repoRoot, and the bounded maxAttempts repair budget), the deliberately two-field advisory TaskImplementerReport ({ claimedComplete, narrative }), and the TaskImplementerAdapter interface with a single implement(req): Promise<TaskImplementerReport>. Types only, no implementation (migration step 3).
acceptanceChecks: schemas.ts exports TaskImplementerRequest, TaskImplementerReport ({ claimedComplete, narrative }), and TaskImplementerAdapter with one method implement(req: TaskImplementerRequest): Promise<TaskImplementerReport>.

TaskImplementerRequest.task is typed against the WORKFLOW PlanTask from src/workflow/artifacts/plan.ts and completedDependencies is readonly BuildTaskOutcome[]; the report is documented and used as advisory-only.

tests.name: schemas: TaskImplementerRequest.task binds to the WORKFLOW PlanTask (src/workflow/artifacts/plan.ts) with completedDependencies typed readonly BuildTaskOutcome[], and TaskImplementerReport exposes only the two advisory fields { claimedComplete, narrative }

tasks.title: Implement TaskImplementerAdapter over one serial CliProvider subprocess (with the UNPROVEN-host finding resolved)
tasks.summary: Implement the sc5 quarantine seam. FIRST resolve the LLD's single load-bearing UNPROVEN question by reading src/agent/providers/cli-provider.ts directly: can its one-document-per-call complete()/completeStructured() surface actually supervise a long free-form editing session, or is provider-level work required? The boundary explicitly reserves that provider-level work as in-scope for s3, so this Task must land an explicit finding and either do the required provider change or scope it before building on top — never assume the happy path. Then implement(): exactly one CliProvider subprocess per Task through the shared LLMProvider abstraction, cloud reached only via the claude/codex CLI binaries (no direct REST), serial by construction and never Promise.all'd. A subprocess/structured-output rejection propagates out of implement() so the sequencer can treat it as an implementer failure; the returned report is advisory only and never advances the run (migration step 4; sc5; invariants c2).
acceptanceChecks: The direct read of src/agent/providers/cli-provider.ts produces an explicit written finding on whether its structured-output/complete path can host the multi-turn free-form editing loop; if it cannot, the required provider-level change is either implemented in this Task or scoped as a named follow-up — the L does not silently absorb an open-ended provider change.

implement() runs exactly one CliProvider subprocess per call through the injected LLMProvider abstraction with no direct REST client constructed, and callers await one call before the next — provider calls are never Promise.all'd.

A subprocess spawn/mid-session error or a completeStructured retry-budget exhaustion surfaces from implement() as a rejected Promise, and the returned TaskImplementerReport is treated purely as advisory (never consulted to advance a Task).

tests.name: adapter: TaskImplementerAdapter.implement() drives exactly one CliProvider subprocess per Task through the injected LLMProvider abstraction (no direct REST client), and a subprocess-spawn / completeStructured retry-budget rejection surfaces from implement() as a rejected Promise

tasks.title: Implement the daemon verifier (test-run + working-tree diff → BuildTestVerdict + filesTouched) behind an injectable seam
tasks.summary: Extract the self-contained daemon-side verifier that the sequencer and both test Tasks assume as an injectable 'fake daemon test-run+diff hook'. It takes the stated test command verbatim from a PlanTask, runs it as a subprocess, captures exit code + summary into BuildTestVerdict ({ command, passed, exitCode, summary }), and takes the git working-tree diff to compute filesTouched — the daemon's OWN authority for a reached outcome, computed independently of the advisory report. Exposes a single narrow interface (the seam t8/t9 stub) and handles the empty/absent stated test command case explicitly (no command → not a passing verdict). Real subprocess/git mechanics live here, not inside sequencer guts (split out of the former t5 L per s3 critique; invariants c1).
acceptanceChecks: The verifier is a self-contained module exposing a single injectable interface that, given a PlanTask and repoRoot, extracts the stated test command verbatim, runs it, and returns BuildTestVerdict + filesTouched computed from the git working-tree diff — the same seam t8/t9 fake instead of reaching a real provider/git.

An empty or absent stated test command yields a non-passing verdict (never a fabricated 'passed'), and filesTouched reflects only the actual working-tree diff; the verdict is computed with no reference to any TaskImplementerReport.

tests.name: verifier: given a PlanTask + repoRoot the injectable verifier extracts the stated test command verbatim, returns BuildTestVerdict + filesTouched from the working-tree diff, and yields a non-passing verdict (never a fabricated 'passed') for an empty/absent command

tasks.title: Implement the private verdict-driven sequencer over the injected verifier seam
tasks.summary: Implement the private sequencer that materializes the pre-validated PlanTask[] work list ONLY when the run is admitted (sc3 BuildAdmissionResult.admitted === true — consumed from s2), walks it topologically with a serial for...of (never Promise.all, even where dependencies would permit parallelism), gates each Task on its dependsOn (emitting BuildTaskUnreached 'blocked' without invoking the adapter when a dependency did not complete), applies the bounded per-Task maxAttempts repair budget, and computes every terminal BuildTaskOutcome from the injected t5 verifier seam (test run + working-tree diff) rather than the advisory report. Types and drives against the TaskImplementerAdapter INTERFACE (t3), not the concrete CliProvider adapter (t4) — no compile or runtime dependency on t4, so it proceeds in parallel with it. Exposes a per-Task-boundary checkpoint hook the wiring Task (t7) drives for durability. Handles the error/edge cases: exhausted budget with a failing verdict → BuildTaskReached 'failed' and the run halts; empty/absent stated test command → failed/not-reached; zero/single/no-op-edit Tasks (former t5 sequencing half after the verifier split; boundary.internal; invariants c1/c3).
acceptanceChecks: The work list is materialized verbatim from the approved plan's PlanTask[] (same ids, order, and dependsOn, nothing added/dropped/reordered) and only when BuildAdmissionResult.admitted === true; the walk is a serial for...of with exactly one Task in flight at any moment.

The sequencer depends only on the TaskImplementerAdapter interface (t3) and the injected verifier seam (t5) — it constructs no concrete CliProvider and has no compile or runtime dependency on t4; each terminal outcome's status/testVerdict/filesTouched comes from the injected verifier, never from the advisory report, and a Task whose dependency ended failed/blocked/not-reached is emitted BuildTaskUnreached 'blocked' with the adapter never invoked.

The per-Task repair budget bounds retries: on exhaustion with a still-failing verdict the Task is BuildTaskReached 'failed' and the run halts on it; an empty/absent stated test command yields failed/not-reached (never completed); zero-Task and single-Task admitted plans finalize correctly; and the sequencer invokes a per-Task-boundary checkpoint hook so t7 can persist incrementally.

tests.name: sequencer: over deterministic in-memory PlanTask[] it materializes the work list verbatim (only when admitted===true), walks it serially topologically, gates dependents to BuildTaskUnreached 'blocked' without invoking the adapter, computes each outcome from the injected verifier seam, and halts on a budget-exhausted failing verdict

tasks.title: Wire the sequencer behind registerBuildRunners, emit outcome progress, and persist the outcome list incrementally
tasks.summary: Drive the sequencer from the build stage's start/decompose/synthesize/finalize turn handlers registered via registerBuildRunners() (sc1, owned by s1) — adding the sequencing turn behind the existing registration without re-registering or mutating sibling stages. Emit BuildTaskOutcome progress at every Task boundary (task-start, implementer-finished, test-verdict, advance-or-halt) through the WorkflowStepOutputBuild / WorkflowProgress driving surface (sc2, owned by s1), keeping a single source of truth for Task status — no second store. Own the durability NFR explicitly: supply the storage-writer implementation behind the sequencer's per-Task-boundary checkpoint hook (t6) so the accumulated BuildTaskOutcome[] is persisted through the existing hash-json/storage writers AT EACH Task boundary, not only at finalize — because build (unlike define/design/plan) mutates the working tree non-transactionally, a daemon restart mid-run must leave a readable record of what already landed. All stage-boundary logging goes through getLogger('workflow:build'), never console.log (migration step 7; interactionWithShared sc1/sc2; observability + durability NFR).
acceptanceChecks: The sequencer runs behind the build stage handlers registered by registerBuildRunners(); no sibling stage (define / design.epic / design.story / tracker) is re-registered or mutated, and BuildTaskOutcome data is emitted at each Task boundary through the WorkflowStepOutputBuild / WorkflowProgress surface with no second source of truth for Task status.

The accumulated BuildTaskOutcome[] is persisted incrementally at each Task boundary via the sequencer's checkpoint hook through the existing hash-json/storage writers (not only at finalize), so a simulated mid-run restart leaves a readable record of the outcomes already landed; halts name the failed Task + reason + blocked set, and all logging uses getLogger('workflow:build').

tests.name: wiring: the sequencing turn runs behind registerBuildRunners() without re-registering sibling stages, emits BuildTaskOutcome at each Task boundary through the WorkflowStepOutputBuild/WorkflowProgress surface, and the checkpoint hook persists the accumulated outcomes at each boundary so a simulated mid-run restart leaves a readable record

tasks.title: Unit-test the private sequencer and verifier seam without a live provider
tasks.summary: Add a node:test suite under src/workflow/runners/build/__tests__ that proves the sequencer over deterministic in-memory inputs, cloning the src/daemon/__tests__/workflow-rpc.test.ts throwing-LLMProvider stub and the src/workflow/__tests__/executor.test.ts harness — no live provider, no real git/test execution. Injects a fake TaskImplementerAdapter and the t5 verifier seam (returning a canned BuildTestVerdict + filesTouched) so nothing spawns. Covers verbatim work-list materialization, topological serial walk, dependency gating (blocked emission with no verdict/filesTouched), the BuildTaskOutcome union invariant, the checkpoint hook firing at each boundary, and the empty-test-command case. Uses chain / independent-pair / dependsOn:[] / single-Task / zero-Task fixtures (migration step 8; testStrategy unit level; acceptance ac1/ac2/ac3/ac4).
acceptanceChecks: A node:test suite runs green under `npx tsx --test` with no live provider and no real git/test execution, cloning the workflow-rpc.test.ts stub and executor.test.ts harness patterns and injecting a fake verifier seam that returns a canned BuildTestVerdict + filesTouched.

It proves verbatim work-list materialization, topological serial walk (independent Tasks still one at a time), dependency gating (blocked BuildTaskUnreached with no verdict/filesTouched), the union invariant, and the per-Task-boundary checkpoint hook firing, across chain / independent-pair / dependsOn:[] / single-Task / zero-Task fixtures.

tests.name: build/__tests__: sequencer+verifier suite over chain / independent-pair / dependsOn:[] / single-Task / zero-Task fixtures with a fake adapter and canned verifier seam — proves verbatim work-list materialization, serial topological walk, dependency gating (blocked, no verdict/filesTouched), the union invariant, and the checkpoint hook firing at each boundary

tasks.title: Integration-test the adapter seam and serial-provider invariants
tasks.summary: Add an integration suite exercising sequencer → TaskImplementerAdapter.implement() → daemon-verdict end to end against a fake CliProvider/LLMProvider (that counts in-flight calls to assert never >1, and can resolve, reject, or emit an advisory report claiming completion regardless of the real verdict) plus the stubbed t5 verifier producing BuildTestVerdict + filesTouched independently of the report. Proves one subprocess per Task, that an advisory claimedComplete:true never advances a failing-verdict Task, that a provider rejection / structured-output failure degrades to a reported implementer failure with the daemon verdict as sole authority, and the sc3 precondition — the adapter is unreachable when admitted !== true, so treeUntouched holds on refusal (migration step 8; testStrategy integration level; acceptance ac3/ac4/ac5).
acceptanceChecks: A concurrency guard on the fake provider (increment-on-entry / assert-never-gt-1) confirms provider calls are strictly serial across the whole build path, and the LLM is reached only through the injected LLMProvider abstraction — no direct REST client is constructed.

An advisory TaskImplementerReport with claimedComplete:true does NOT advance a Task whose daemon verdict fails, and a rejected/structured-failure provider Promise degrades to a reported implementer failure with the daemon verdict as the sole authority.
The sc3 admission gate is exercised: the adapter is unreachable when BuildAdmissionResult.admitted !== true and treeUntouched holds on refusal.

tests.name: build/__tests__: sequencer→adapter.implement()→daemon-verdict end-to-end against a fake CliProvider/LLMProvider (in-flight counter asserts never >1) and stubbed verifier — proves one subprocess per Task, advisory claimedComplete:true never advances a failing-verdict Task, a provider rejection degrades to a reported implementer failure, and the adapter is unreachable when admitted!==true (treeUntouched on refusal)
